story.title: Checkout order summary shows the correct item total, tax, and total
story.description: A shopper completes the two-step checkout (enter information, review order summary, finish). The order-summary step must show the correct item total (sum of cart item prices), the tax, and the total (item total + tax), so the shopper sees an accurate amount before committing.

acceptance_criteria: Given a shopper with two known items in the cart, when they proceed to checkout and submit valid first name, last name, and postal code, then they reach the "Checkout: Overview" step showing both items.
Given the checkout overview step, when the order summary is displayed, then the "Item total" equals the exact sum of the unit prices of the items in the cart.
Given the checkout overview step, when the order summary is displayed, then the "Total" equals the displayed "Item total" plus the displayed "Tax" (the arithmetic on screen is internally consistent).

Given the checkout information step, when the shopper submits the form with a missing required field (e.g. no postal code), then they remain on the information step and an error message is shown — they do not advance to the overview.
Given the checkout overview step, when the shopper clicks "Finish", then they reach the "Checkout: Complete!" confirmation and the cart badge is cleared (the order has been placed).

risks.description: The order summary displays a wrong item total (not the sum of the cart items' unit prices), misrepresenting the amount the shopper is about to pay.
The displayed total is not internally consistent with the shown item total plus tax, so the on-screen arithmetic does not add up.
The information form advances to the overview despite a missing required field, letting an order proceed with incomplete shipping data.
Finishing the order does not reach the confirmation or does not clear the cart, leaving the shopper unsure whether the order was placed.
